diagrams.title: Cyber Attack Lifecycle (Kill Chain)
diagrams.description: Shows the seven sequential stages of a typical cyber attack from initial reconnaissance to data exfiltration, helping learners understand how attacks progress and where defensive controls can be deployed.
diagrams.purpose: Explains the attack kill chain concept and identifies intervention points for security controls

diagrams.title: Threat Categories and Attack Vectors
diagrams.description: Illustrates the main categories of security threats and their delivery methods (attack vectors) showing how different threats infiltrate target systems and networks.
diagrams.purpose: Categorizes different threat types and shows how they enter and attack systems

diagrams.title: CIA Triad with Threat Mapping
diagrams.description: Presents the foundational CIA triad (Confidentiality, Integrity, Availability) with concrete threat examples mapped to each principle, showing which attacks target which security goal.
diagrams.purpose: Maps common threats to the fundamental information security principles
